payload.answer: Captured architectural intent: Brain DB as source of truth, Cognee as rebuildable semantic projection, Slack as strict memory intake agent; recorded Telegram addition and model cost/performance as open questions.
payload.decision: No explicit decisions recorded beyond stated architectural intent and open questions; only capture role assignments and open questions as provisional.

entity_resolution.reason: The summary does not provide identifiers beyond component names; preserve as textual entity names.

payload.intent: Extract durable memory cards from the provided chat summary context for Brain evaluation.

memory_cards.statement: Brain DB is intended to be the source of truth for the system.
Cognee is intended to be a rebuildable semantic projection derived from Brain DB.
Slack is intended to be a strict memory intake agent.
Whether Telegram should be added later is an open question.
Which model is cheapest while saturating performance is an open question.

details: Only statements present in the provided context were converted into memory cards.
Open questions were preserved as open questions without choosing answers.

repair_options: Add explicit definitions for 'strict' memory intake behavior for Slack (e.g., validation rules, deduping, timestamps).
Document the rebuild procedure/trigger semantics for Cognee from Brain DB (e.g., batch vs real-time, consistency guarantees).
Record evaluation criteria for the 'cheapest model while saturating performance' open question (latency, cost, quality metrics, throughput).
If Telegram is later added, capture scope (read-only vs write), mapping to intake policy, and initial rollout conditions.
